caption: The video appears to be an educational lecture on cryogenic engineering, specifically focusing on assignments related to the Linde-Hampson system. The first frame presents an assignment that requires determining various parameters for a Linde-Hampson system using air as the working fluid, operating between 1.013 bar (1 atm) and 202.6 bar (200 atm) at 300 K, with a heat exchanger effectiveness of 100%. The parameters to calculate include ideal work requirement, liquid yield, work per unit mass compressed, work per unit mass liquefied, and the figure of merit (FOM). The second frame is similar but changes the working fluid from air to oxygen, while keeping all other conditions and required calculations the same. The lecture is delivered by Prof. M D Atrey from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Bombay, as indicated at the bottom of each slide. The content suggests a detailed analysis of cryogenic systems under different working fluids.
